info.name: Cheerio Direct APIs
info.description:
BASE_API: https://newprod.api.cheerio.in/direct-apis

Hey there developer, glad to have you visit our API integration page. Here’s all you need to get started.

The intent of this API is to ensure that any third party can send messages to their customers via Email, SMS or WhatsApp utilising the Cheerio API.

There are few pre-requisites as follows:

  1. You can generate an API key in Manage Profile.

  2. A WhatsApp Business Account which is already live: Visit here to read documentation on it or reach out to ritul@cheerio.in

  3. Templates you wish to send should be already submitted on your Cheerio Dashboard

  4. Email Sender ID Details - such as domain, sender email ID and authorisation. You can create a Sender ID in Cheerio Dashboard => Email => Manage Sender Id.

  5. Purchase 6 month Cheerio premium subscription plan

item.name: WhatsApp

request.description: To send a whatsapp message with media such as video, document or image, we need to use this method and create a media ID.
To send a whatsapp message with media such as video, document or image, we need to use this method and create a media ID.

Find more information about body here https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/on-premises/reference/messages#template-object

Send template is used to open a business intitiated utility or marketing conversation where the sent message corresponds to a template already approved by meta for your Whatsapp business account. To read about how to use Send Template Message please refer to

https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/on-premises/reference/messages#template-object

This covers all information and variables for send template body.

Field Type Required Description
to string Yes WhatsApp ID or phone number of the recipient.
type string Yes Type of message to send. Example: template.
template object Yes Template message object.

template Object

Field Type Required Description
namespace string No Template namespace.
language object Yes Language configuration for the template.
name string Yes Name of the approved template.
components array No Array of template components with parameters.

language Object

Field Type Required Description
code string Yes Language code. Example: en_US.

components Object

Field Type Required Description
type string Yes Supported values: header, body, button.
parameters array Conditional Required when type=button. Array of parameters for the component.

parameters Object

Field Type Required Description
type string Yes Parameter type. Values: text, currency, date_time, image, document, video.
text string Conditional Required when type=text. Parameter text value.

Response (200 OK)

Field Type Description
messaging_product string Messaging platform name.
contacts array Recipient contact details.
contacts[].input string Input number sent in request.
contacts[].wa_id string WhatsApp ID of recipient.
messages array Message delivery details.
messages[].id string Unique message ID.

Example Response

{
  "messaging_product": "whatsapp",
  "contacts": [
    {
      "input": "918198776153",
      "wa_id": "918198776153"
    }
  ],
  "messages": [
    {
      "id": "wamid.HBgMOTE4MTE2ODU2M8865IAERgSMUEwRUM5ODIxRTFGMzZBQjk2AA=="
    }
  ]
}

This endpoint allows you to send a WhatsApp message to a specific recipient. The request should be sent as an HTTP POST to /v1/whatsapp/direct/send.

Please go through the documentation below to get a better understanding of the BODY

https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/on-premises/guides/messages

Request Body

  • recipient_type: Specifies the type of recipient, in this case "individual".
  • to: The WhatsApp ID of the recipient.
  • type: Specifies the type of interactive message, in this case "interactive".

Response

Upon a successful execution, the response will have a status code of 200 and a content type of application/json. The response body will contain:

  • status: Indicates the status of the operation, where 0 denotes success.
  • flag: A boolean flag indicating the status.
  • data: Contains details about the messaging product and contacts.
    • messaging_product: The messaging product used.
    • contacts: An array containing input and WhatsApp ID details.
      • input: Input details.
      • wa_id: WhatsApp ID.
    • messages: An array of message IDs.
      • id: The ID of the sent message.
  • message: Additional information or message related to the operation.

Send a message to sender number from recipient number. You can only initiate a conversation and send a message using template message. Direct messages can be sent once a conversation is active and user has replied or if user initiates the conversation.

Create template API is used to create whatsapp templates.

Find more information about body and validations here https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/business-management-api/message-templates/

This covers all information and variables for send template body.

Use below code in buttons to get trackable links. Analytics will be visible in Cheerio Dashboard

                {
                    "type": "URL",
                    "text": "Visit Website",
                    "url": "https://web.cheerio.in/ss/{{1}}",
                    "example": [
                        "https://web.cheerio.in/ss/my_companyname"
                    ]
                }

Create Bulk template API is used to create whatsapp templates in bulk.

Send an array of objects in body in meta described format. Max length of array can be 500.

Find more information about body and validations here https://developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp/business-management-api/message-templates/

This covers all information and variables for send template body.

Use below code in buttons to get trackable links. Analytics will be visible in Cheerio Dashboard

                {
                    "type": "URL", 
                    "text": "Visit Website", //Can be changed
                    "url": "https://web.cheerio.in/ss/{{1}}",
                    "example": [
                        "https://web.cheerio.in/ss/my_companyname"
                    ]
                }

This endpoint allows you to send multiple WhatsApp messages.

Request Body

  • The request should be a JSON array with each object containing the following parameters:
    • messaging_product: (string) The messaging product to use.
    • to: (string) The recipient's phone number.
    • type: (string) The type of message.
    • template: (object) The template object with the following sub-parameters:
      • name: (string) The name of the template.
      • language: (object) The language object with the following sub-parameter:
        • code: (string) The language code.

Response

Upon successful execution, the response will have a status code of 200 and a JSON object with the following parameter:

  • message: (string) A message indicating the status of the operation.

The endpoint /v1/getAllTemplates is a GET request that retrieves a list of templates. The response is in JSON format and has the following schema:

{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "total": {
      "type": "integer"
    },
    "data": {
      "type": "array",
      "items": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "id": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "category": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "components": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "type": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "format": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "example": {
                  "type": "object",
                  "properties": {
                    "header_handle": {
                      "type": "array",
                      "items": {
                        "type": "string"
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          },
          "language": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "name": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "quality_score": {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
              "score": {
                "type": "string"
              },
              "date": {
                "type": "integer"
              }
            }
          },
          "rejected_reason": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "status": {
            "type": "string"
          }
        }
      }
    },
    "paginate": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "before": {
          "type": "string"
        },
        "after": {
          "type": "string"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

1. Overview

API Name

Fetch WhatsApp Message Status

Description

This API fetches the current delivery status of a WhatsApp message using its unique WhatsApp Message ID (wamid). It is a read-only, synchronous endpoint intended for polling message delivery state after sending a WhatsApp message.

2. Base Information

Attribute Value
Protocol HTTPS
Base URL https://newprod.api.cheerio.in
API Version v1
Environment Production
Rate Limit 15 requests / second
Authentication API Key
Content-Type application/json

3. Endpoint Definition

HTTP Method

GET

Endpoint Path

/direct-apis/v1/whatsapp-status/:wamid

Full URL Example

https://newprod.api.cheerio.in/direct-apis/v1/whatsapp-status/wamid.HBgKOTM0MDA0NTQ0NxUCABEYEkQ3MEU5MkU1MTE0MTE3OUY5NAA=

4. Path Parameters

Parameter Type Required Description
wamid string ✅ Yes Unique WhatsApp Message ID whose delivery status is being fetched

Example wamid

wamid.HBgKOTM0MDA0NTQ0NxUCABEYEkQ3MEU5MkU1MTE0MTE3OUY5NAA=

5. Headers

Required Headers

Header Value Required Description
x-api-key ✅ Yes Static API key for authentication
Content-Type application/json ✅ Yes Declares response format

Example

x-api-key: your_static_api_key_here
Content-Type: application/json

6. Authentication

Type

API Key Authentication

Header Location

x-api-key

Characteristics

  • Static key

  • Mandatory for every request

  • No OAuth / JWT involved

Postman Recommendation

Store the API key as:

  • Environment Variable
{{x_api_key}}

7. Request Body

❌ No request body

  • This endpoint does not accept or require a JSON payload.

  • Any body sent will be ignored or may result in validation errors depending on server enforcement.

8. Sample cURL (Final)

curl --location --request GET \
  'https://newprod.api.cheerio.in/direct-apis/v1/whatsapp-status/wamid.HBgKOTM0MDA0NTQ0NxUCABEYEkQ3MEU5MkU1MTE0MTE3OUY5NAA=' \
  --header 'x-api-key: YOUR_STATIC_API_KEY' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json'

10. Response Handling

  • 200

  • 404 – Message not found

  • 401 – Invalid API key

  • 400 - Something went wrong

  • 429 – Rate limit exceeded

11. Rate Limiting

Rule Value
Max Requests 15 req/sec
Scope Per API key
Burst Allowed Not specified

Recommendation

  • Avoid polling aggressively

  • Implement exponential backoff on 429 (when enabled)

12. Idempotency & Behavior

Aspect Behavior
Idempotent ✅ Yes
Side Effects ❌ None
Data Mutations ❌ No
Caching Not specified

13. Common Use Cases

  • Check if a WhatsApp message is:

    • Sent

    • Delivered

    • Read

    • Failed

  • Delivery tracking dashboards

  • Message audit logs

  • Retry decision logic

14. Error Scenarios (Conceptual)

Scenario Cause
Invalid wamid Malformed or non-existent message ID
Unauthorized Missing or invalid API key
Rate Limit More than 15 req/sec
Server Error Internal WhatsApp or API gateway failure

15. Best Practices

  • Always validate wamid before calling

  • Use environment variables in Postman

  • Do not hardcode API keys

  • Respect rate limits

  • Log responses for reconciliation

Get WhatsApp Template Details

This endpoint retrieves the details of a specific WhatsApp template by providing the template name or ID in the URL.

To use this API with template name, you need to do sync templates first. In Cheerio Dashboard go to Template Library>Whatsapp>Sync

Request

  • Method: GET

  • URL: /v1/whatsapp/template/:template_name_or_id

Parameters

  • template_name_or_id (in the URL path): The name or ID of the WhatsApp template for which details are to be retrieved.

Response

Upon a successful execution, the endpoint returns a JSON object with the following fields:

  • _id: The unique ID of the template.

  • id: The ID of the template.

  • category: The category of the template.

  • components: An array of components within the template, including type, example, and buttons.

  • createdAt: The timestamp of when the template was created.

  • language: The language of the template.

  • name: The name of the template.

  • quality_score: The quality score of the template, including the score and date.

  • rejected_reason: The reason for rejection, if applicable.

  • status: The status of the template.

  • updatedAt: The timestamp of when the template was last updated.

Example

{
    "_id": "",
    "id": "",
    "category": "",
    "components": [
        {
            "type": "",
            "example": {
                "header_handle": [""],
                "body_text": [],
                "_id": ""
            },
            "_id": "",
            "buttons": []
        }
    ],
    "createdAt": "",
    "language": "",
    "name": "",
    "quality_score": {
        "score": "",
        "date": 0,
        "_id": ""
    },
    "rejected_reason": "",
    "status": "",
    "updatedAt": ""
}

item.name: Contacts

formdata.fileNotInWorkingDirectoryWarning: This file isn't in your working directory. Teammates you share this request with won't be able to use this file. To make collaboration easier you can setup your working directory in Settings.

request.description:
Create a contacts label for all customers you wish to send announcement or a campaign. To start with create a CSV file with columns: Name, Email and Phone Number. Country Code if applicable can be added as an optional column, default country code is ‘+91’.

Go to the location as mentioned in below curl and use the API key we have provided to you in the header.

Mention the file path, your user ID and contact label name and your contact label will be created.

Update Contact Details

An HTTP PUT request is made to update the details of a contact with the given phone number.

Request URL

/v1/contacts/updateDetails/919779003936?ref=phone

Request Params

  • ref: (string, required): "phone" or "id"

Request Body

The request body is of type raw and contains the following parameters:

  • name (string, optional): The name of the contact.

  • customAttribute (string, optional): Custom attribute for the contact.

    • Can change the name "customAttribute" to any variable name.

    • A new attribute needs to be created for atleast 1 contact in Cheerio Dashboard before updating the attribute through API. Once created you can add the attribute for all the contacts through API.

Response

The response is in JSON format with the following schema:

{
    "_id": STRING,
    "mobile": STRING,
    "customData": OBJECT,
    "name": STRING,
    "email": STRING
}

Response Status

  • Status: 200

  • Content-Type: application/json

Response Documentation

The response of this request is documented as a JSON schema.

This API endpoint allows you to delete a label based on its ID or name. The label to be deleted is specified in the URL path parameter.

To delete a label, send an HTTP DELETE request to /v1/labels/:option/:id_or_name, where :option can be either "id" or "name", and :id_or_name is the ID or name of the label you want to delete.

Request Parameters

  • :option (path parameter): Specifies whether the label will be identified by its ID or name. Possible values are "id" or "name".
  • :id_or_name (path parameter): The ID or name of the label to be deleted.

Response

The API response will have a status code indicating the success of the request. A successful deletion will return a status code of 200.

The response body will contain the following fields:

  • status: A code indicating the status of the request. A value of 0 indicates success.
  • flag: A boolean value indicating whether the label was successfully deleted. In this case, it will be true.
  • data: An object containing additional data related to the deletion. In this case, it will contain the ID of the deleted label (deletedLabelId).
  • message: A message related to the deletion. In this case, it will be an empty string.

Please note that the specific values of the deleted label ID or any personal information are not included in the response for security reasons.

Example request:

DELETE /v1/labels/id/12345

Example response:

Status: 200
{
  "status": 0,
  "flag": true,
  "data": {
    "deletedLabelId": ""
  },
  "message": ""
}

description.content: id or name of the label to be deleted

request.description:
GET /v1/contact/getAll

This endpoint is used to retrieve all contacts based on the provided search criteria.

Request

  • Method: GET
  • Endpoint: /v1/contact/getAll
  • Query Parameters:
    • search (string, optional): The search criteria to filter the contacts.

Response

  • Content-Type: application/json
  • 200 OK: The response will contain an array of contact objects matching the search criteria.

Response

  • status (number) - The status of the response.

  • flag (boolean) - Indicates the status of the operation.

  • data (object) - Contains the retrieved contacts data.

    • docs (array) - An array of contact objects.

      • _id (string) - The ID of the contact.

      • userId (string) - The ID of the user associated with the contact.

      • mobile (string) - The mobile number of the contact.

      • uid_code (string) - The UID code of the contact.

      • status (boolean) - The status of the contact.

      • createdAt (string) - The creation date of the contact.

      • updatedAt (string) - The last update date of the contact.

      • conversationId (string) - The ID of the conversation associated with the contact.

      • email (string) - The email address of the contact.

      • name (string) - The name of the contact.

      • stop (boolean) - Indicates if the contact is stopped.

    • totalDocs (number) - The total number of contacts retrieved.

    • limit (number) - The limit of contacts per page.

    • totalPages (number) - The total number of pages.

    • page (number) - The current page number.

    • pagingCounter (number) - The paging counter.

    • hasPrevPage (boolean) - Indicates if there is a previous page.

    • hasNextPage (boolean) - Indicates if there is a next page.

    • prevPage (number) - The previous page number.

    • nextPage (number) - The next page number.

    • message (string) - Any additional message related to the response.

Upload Single Contact

This endpoint allows you to upload a single contact to the Cheerio.

Request Body

  • name (string, required): The name of the contact.

  • mobile (string, required): The mobile number of the contact.

  • email (string, required): The email address of the contact.

  • customData (object, optional): Additional custom data for the contact

  • labels (array, required): An array of labels associated with the contact.

Response

The response for this request can be documented as a JSON schema.

{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "status": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "message": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "data": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "contactId": {
          "type": "string"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

item.description: Send a message to your customers via adding them as contacts on Cheerio, through Upload CSV method.

item.name: Announcements

request.description:
data: {
    campaignName: string;
    templateName: string;
    tagIdArr?: string[]; // either tagIdArr or labelName is requires
    labelName?: string;
    header?: {
      type: "document" | "video" | "image" | "text";
      text?: string;
      mediaId?: string;
      fileName?: string; // Only for documents
    };
    cta?: [{
      index: number,
      type: "url";
      value: string;
    }];
    bodyParameters?: string[];
    language_code: string;
    mpm?: {
      index: number,
      thumbnail_product_retailer_id: string;
      sections: Array<{
        title: string;
        product_items: Array<{
          product_retailer_id: string;
        }>;
      }>;
    };
  },

Send Whatsapp Announcement is used to send messages in bulk to your customers. For Cheerio you need to mention mandatory parameters as below:

  1. labelName: Name of uploaded CSV contact list
  2. campaignName : Name of Campaign.
  3. templateName : Name of approved template to be used
  4. language_code: Language of template that you are using
  5. CTA : Optional : If you have a click to website button in your template
  6. header: Optional base don template type: Text, Image, Video, File

This API endpoint allows you to send a labeled email announcement. The HTTP POST request should be made to /v1/announcements/label-email/send.

The response body contains the following information:

{
    "status": 200,
    "flag": true,
    "data": {
        "balance": {
            "sms": number,
            "whatsapp": number,
            "email": number
        },
        "emailCount": number
    },
    "message": string
}

The response body includes the following properties:

  • status: An integer indicating the status of the response.
  • flag: A boolean value indicating the success or failure of the request.
  • data: An object containing additional data related to the request.
    • balance: An object representing the balance of different communication channels (SMS, WhatsApp, and email). Each channel has a corresponding count value.
    • emailCount: An integer representing the count of email announcements.
  • message: A string containing any additional message or information related to the request.

Please ensure that you provide the necessary request parameters and headers as required by the API endpoint.

item.description: Send announcement in bulk using these API call after you have finished the previous steps.

item.name: Email

request.description:
Request Headers

Parameter Type Required Description
x-api-key string required Your Cheerio API key for authentication.
Content-Type string required Must be application/json.

Request Body

Parameter Type Required Description
userId string required The unique identifier of the recipient user in Cheerio.
email string required The recipient's email address (e.g., user@example.com).
subject string required The subject line of the email.
campaignName string required A label for the campaign. Used for tracking and analytics.
htmlContent string optional Raw HTML content to use as the email body. One of htmlContent or emailBuilderId is required.
emailBuilderId string optional ID of a pre-built email template in Cheerio. One of htmlContent or emailBuilderId is required.

**
⚠️  Conditional requirement: Exactly one of htmlContent or emailBuilderId must be provided. If both are supplied, htmlContent takes precedence.**

This API endpoint allows you to send a single email announcement.

To send an email announcement, make a POST request to /v1/announcements/single-email/send.

The request should include the necessary parameters for sending the email, such as the recipient's email address, subject, and content.

The response will include the status of the request, a flag indicating whether the email was sent successfully, the balance of available resources (SMS, WhatsApp, and email), and the recipient's email address.

Here is an example of the response received from the last execution:

Status: 200
{
  "status": 0,
  "flag": true,
  "data": {
    "balance": {
      "sms": 0,
      "whatsapp": 0,
      "email": 0
    },
    "sentTo": ""
  },
  "message": ""
}

Please note that the values provided in the response are placeholders and may not reflect the actual data.

item.description: List of all API calls to carry out Sending Email to a single contact.

request.description:
This API endpoint allows you to retrieve the summary analytics for a specific ID.

To make a GET request to this endpoint, you need to provide the ID in the URL path. The base URL for the request is /v1/analytics/summary/:id, where :id should be replaced with the actual ID you want to retrieve the summary for.

The response to this request will have a status code of 200 if the request was successful. The response body will be in JSON format and will contain the following properties:

  • status: An integer indicating the status of the request. In this case, the value is 0.
  • flag: A boolean value indicating whether the request was successful or not. In this case, the value is true.
  • data: An object containing the summary analytics data. It has the following properties:
    • announcement: An object containing information about the announcement. It has the following properties:
      • template_name: The name of the template used for the announcement.
      • template_Id: The ID of the template used for the announcement.
      • date: The date of the announcement.
      • label_name: The name of the label associated with the announcement.
      • label: The label associated with the announcement.
      • email_arr: An array of email addresses.
      • mobile_arr: An array of mobile numbers.
      • tag_id_arr: An array of tag IDs.
    • email_stats: An object containing email statistics. It has the following properties:
      • queued: The number of emails queued.
      • sent: The number of emails sent.
      • delivery: The number of emails delivered.
      • opened: The number of emails opened.
      • Unsubscribe: The number of email unsubscribe requests.
      • requests: The number of email requests.
      • invalid: The number of invalid email addresses.
      • link_CTR: The click-through rate for email links.
      • link_ids: An array of link IDs.
      • link_Clicks: The number of email link clicks.
      • email_url: The URL for the email.
      • total: The total number of emails.
      • deliveryRate: The delivery rate for emails.
      • openedRate: The open rate for emails.
      • sentRate: The send rate for emails.
    • whatsapp_stats: An object containing WhatsApp statistics. It has the following properties:
      • queued: The number of WhatsApp messages queued.
      • sent: The number of WhatsApp messages sent.
      • delivery: The number of WhatsApp messages delivered.
      • invalid: The number of invalid WhatsApp numbers.
      • opened: The number of WhatsApp messages opened.
      • link_CTR: The click-through rate for WhatsApp links.
      • link_ids: An array of link IDs.
      • link_Clicks: The number of WhatsApp link clicks.
      • whatsapp_url: The URL for WhatsApp.
      • total: The total number of WhatsApp messages.
      • button_click: An array of button clicks.
      • deliveryRate: The delivery rate for WhatsApp messages.
      • openedRate: The open rate for WhatsApp messages.
      • sentRate: The send rate for WhatsApp messages.
    • sms: An object containing SMS statistics. It has the following properties:
      • total: The total number of SMS messages.
      • delivery: The number of SMS messages delivered.
      • invalid: The number of invalid SMS numbers.
      • queued: The number of SMS messages queued.
      • link_CTR: The click-through rate for SMS links.
      • link_ids: An array of link IDs.
      • link_Clicks: The number of SMS link clicks.
      • sms_url: The URL for SMS messages.
      • opened: The number of SMS messages opened.
      • sent: The number of SMS messages sent.
      • deliveryRate: The delivery rate for SMS messages.
      • sentRate: The send rate for SMS messages.
    • shopify: An object containing Shopify data. It has the following properties:
      • days: The number of days.
    • _id: The ID of the summary.
    • user_id: The ID of the user.
    • label_column: The label column.
    • createdAt: The date and time the summary was created.
    • updatedAt: The date and time the summary was last updated.
    • __v: The version of the summary.
  • message: A message related to the request.

Please note that specific values like names, emails, and URLs may not be included in this description for security and privacy reasons.

This API endpoint allows you to retrieve the summary analytics for a specific ID.

To make a GET request to this endpoint, you need to provide the ID in the URL path. The base URL for the request is /v1/analytics/summary/:id, where :id should be replaced with the actual ID you want to retrieve the summary for.

The response to this request will have a status code of 200 if the request was successful. The response body will be in JSON format and will contain the following properties:

  • status: An integer indicating the status of the request. In this case, the value is 0.
  • flag: A boolean value indicating whether the request was successful or not. In this case, the value is true.
  • data: An object containing the summary analytics data. It has the following properties:
    • announcement: An object containing information about the announcement. It has the following properties:
      • template_name: The name of the template used for the announcement.
      • template_Id: The ID of the template used for the announcement.
      • date: The date of the announcement.
      • label_name: The name of the label associated with the announcement.
      • label: The label associated with the announcement.
      • email_arr: An array of email addresses.
      • mobile_arr: An array of mobile numbers.
      • tag_id_arr: An array of tag IDs.
    • email_stats: An object containing email statistics. It has the following properties:
      • queued: The number of emails queued.
      • sent: The number of emails sent.
      • delivery: The number of emails delivered.
      • opened: The number of emails opened.
      • Unsubscribe: The number of email unsubscribe requests.
      • requests: The number of email requests.
      • invalid: The number of invalid email addresses.
      • link_CTR: The click-through rate for email links.
      • link_ids: An array of link IDs.
      • link_Clicks: The number of email link clicks.
      • email_url: The URL for the email.
      • total: The total number of emails.
      • deliveryRate: The delivery rate for emails.
      • openedRate: The open rate for emails.
      • sentRate: The send rate for emails.
    • whatsapp_stats: An object containing WhatsApp statistics. It has the following properties:
      • queued: The number of WhatsApp messages queued.
      • sent: The number of WhatsApp messages sent.
      • delivery: The number of WhatsApp messages delivered.
      • invalid: The number of invalid WhatsApp numbers.
      • opened: The number of WhatsApp messages opened.
      • link_CTR: The click-through rate for WhatsApp links.
      • link_ids: An array of link IDs.
      • link_Clicks: The number of WhatsApp link clicks.
      • whatsapp_url: The URL for WhatsApp.
      • total: The total number of WhatsApp messages.
      • button_click: An array of button clicks.
      • deliveryRate: The delivery rate for WhatsApp messages.
      • openedRate: The open rate for WhatsApp messages.
      • sentRate: The send rate for WhatsApp messages.
    • sms: An object containing SMS statistics. It has the following properties:
      • total: The total number of SMS messages.
      • delivery: The number of SMS messages delivered.
      • invalid: The number of invalid SMS numbers.
      • queued: The number of SMS messages queued.
      • link_CTR: The click-through rate for SMS links.
      • link_ids: An array of link IDs.
      • link_Clicks: The number of SMS link clicks.
      • sms_url: The URL for SMS messages.
      • opened: The number of SMS messages opened.
      • sent: The number of SMS messages sent.
      • deliveryRate: The delivery rate for SMS messages.
      • sentRate: The send rate for SMS messages.
    • shopify: An object containing Shopify data. It has the following properties:
      • days: The number of days.
    • _id: The ID of the summary.
    • user_id: The ID of the user.
    • label_column: The label column.
    • createdAt: The date and time the summary was created.
    • updatedAt: The date and time the summary was last updated.
    • __v: The version of the summary.
  • message: A message related to the request.

Please note that specific values like names, emails, and URLs may not be included in this description for security and privacy reasons.

request.url: /v1/announcements/single-sms/send?SenderId=612616&Is_Unicode=false&Is_Flash=false&Message={%23var%23} brings to you an exclusive range of birthday celebration products via Cheerio. View Now {%23var%23}	&MobileNumbers=919779003936
request.description:
This API endpoint sends a single SMS announcement using the GET method. The request should include the SenderId, Is_Unicode, Is_Flash, Message, and MobileNumbers as query parameters. The SenderId specifies the sender's ID, Is_Unicode indicates whether the message is in Unicode (set true for foreign languages), Is_Flash specifies if the message is a flash message, Message contains the text of the SMS, and MobileNumbers includes the recipient's mobile numbers.

Sms works on templates. You have to get the template approve first. Once you are done testing let us know the exact message you want to sent. We will get it approved from TRAI. Message can contain 2 params. Param’s max length can be 30 chars

query.value: {#var#} brings to you an exclusive range of birthday celebration products via Cheerio. View Now {#var#}

request.description:
GET /v1/workflows

The endpoint /v1/workflows is a GET request that retrieves a list of workflows.

Request

This request does not require a request body.

Response

The response is in JSON format and has the following schema:

{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "status": {
      "type": "integer"
    },
    "flag": {
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "data": {
      "type": "array",
      "items": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
          "kylasDetails": {
            "type": "object",
            "properties": {
              "attributeList": {
                "type": "array"
              }
            }
          },
          "_id": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "userId": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "nodes": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "id": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "type": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "position": {
                  "type": "object",
                  "properties": {
                    "x": {
                      "type": "integer"
                    },
                    "y": {
                      "type": "integer"
                    }
                  }
                },
                "data": {
                  "type": "object",
                  "properties": {
                    "label": {
                      "type": "string"
                    }
                  }
                },
                "width": {
                  "type": "integer"
                },
                "height": {
                  "type": "integer"
                },
                "selected": {
                  "type": "boolean"
                },
                "positionAbsolute": {
                  "type": "object",
                  "properties": {
                    "x": {
                      "type": "integer"
                    },
                    "y": {
                      "type": "integer"
                    }
                  }
                },
                "dragging": {
                  "type": "boolean"
                }
              }
            }
          },
          "edges": {
            "type": "array",
            "items": {
              "type": "object",
              "properties": {
                "source": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "sourceHandle": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "target": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "targetHandle": {
                  "type": "string"
                },
                "id": {
                  "type": "string"
                }
              }
            }
          },
          "name": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "status": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "shopify": {
            "type": "boolean"
          },
          "woo": {
            "type": "boolean"
          },
          "campaignType": {
            "type": "boolean"
          },
          "createdAt": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "updatedAt": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "__v": {
            "type": "integer"
          },
          "defaultWorkflowNode": {
            "type": "string"
          }
        }
      }
    },
    "message": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  }
}

GET /v1/workflow/:id

This endpoint retrieves a specific workflow by its ID.

Request

This request does not require a request body.

Response

The response is in JSON format and has the following schema:

{
  "status": "integer",
  "flag": "boolean",
  "data": {
    "kylasDetails": {
      "attributeList": "array"
    },
    "_id": "string",
    "userId": "string",
    "nodes": "array",
    "edges": "array",
    "name": "string",
    "status": "string",
    "shopify": "boolean",
    "woo": "boolean",
    "campaignType": "boolean",
    "createdAt": "string",
    "updatedAt": "string",
    "__v": "integer",
    "defaultWorkflowNode": "string"
  },
  "message": "string"
}

Manual Trigger Workflow

This endpoint allows you to manually trigger a workflow.

Request

  • Method: POST

  • URL: /v1/manualTriggerWorkflow

Request Headers

Parameter Type Required Description
x-api-key string required Your Cheerio API key for authentication.
Content-Type string required Must be application/json.

Request Body

Parameter Type Required Description
phoneNumber **string null** optional
email **string null** optional
workflowId string required The unique ID of the workflow to trigger. Retrieve workflow IDs via GET /v1/getWorkflows.
userName string required Display name of the user triggering the workflow. Used for logging and audit purposes.
otherData object optional Any additional custom key-value data to pass into the workflow for personalization or branching logic.

⚠️ Contact identification: At least one of phoneNumber or email is required to identify the contact in Cheerio. Both may be provided.

Response

The response is in JSON format and follows the schema below:

{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "status": {
      "type": "number"
    },
    "flag": {
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "data": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "userId": {
          "type": "string"
        },
        "workflowId": {
          "type": "string"
        },
        "currentNodeId": {
          "type": "string"
        },
        "userUniqueKey": {
          "type": "string"
        },
        "assignedToBot": {
          "type": "boolean"
        },
        "askedForMail": {
          "type": "boolean"
        },
        "onAnalyticDecision": {
          "type": "boolean"
        },
        "status": {
          "type": "string"
        },
        "selfAssigned": {
          "type": ["null", "string"]
        },
        "_id": {
          "type": "string"
        },
        "createdAt": {
          "type": "string"
        },
        "updatedAt": {
          "type": "string"
        },
        "__v": {
          "type": "number"
        }
      }
    },
    "message": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  }
}

Response

This endpoint does not return a response body on success.

HTTP Status Meaning
200 Workflow triggered successfully. No response body is returned.
400 Bad request. Missing required fields or invalid parameter format.
401 Unauthorized. The x-api-key header is missing or invalid.
404 Workflow not found. The provided workflowId does not exist.

Delete all uploaded whatsapp media (Available on Request)

This endpoint is used to send a request for whatsapp media deletion.

Response

The response of this request is a JSON schema that includes the following properties:

  • status (string): The status of the request, indicating success or failure.

  • message (string): A message providing details about the request status.
